sanskrit: ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः । अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥
hindiTranslation: परन्तु जो कर्मोंको मेरे अर्पण करके और मेरे परायण होकर अनन्ययोगसे मेरा ही ध्यान करते हुए मेरी उपासना करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: But those who surrender all actions to Me, take Me as their highest aim, and worship Me with single-pointed devotion, meditating on Me alone.
essence: Total devotion turns every action into worship.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People who give all their actions to Krishna, make him their highest aim, and worship him with single-minded devotion are described here.
simpleInsight: There is relief in not carrying your actions alone; devotion turns effort into offering.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna explains the deepest form of devotion. After describing subtler worship, Krishna now names the devotee who places every action at his feet and keeps a single, undivided focus on him.
modernRelevance: You finish a hard conversation, then stop replaying it for approval. The action was yours to give; the burden of ownership can be laid down.
translationLiteral: ये (those who) / तु (but) / सर्वाणि (all) / कर्माणि (actions) / मयि (in Me) / संन्यस्य (having renounced, offered) / मत्पराः (those devoted to Me as the highest) / अनन्येन (with no other) / एव (indeed) / योगेन (with yoga) / माम् (Me) / ध्यायन्तः (meditating) / उपासते (worship)
